issues_fixed.description: tryFixViolation switch cases used wrong truncated regra strings — none ever matched. Backtrack mechanism was a silent no-op.
issues_fixed.fix_applied: Updated all 5 switch cases to full prefixed strings: 'MAX_DIAS_CONSECUTIVOS' -> 'H1_MAX_DIAS_CONSECUTIVOS', 'DESCANSO_ENTRE_JORNADAS' -> 'H2_DESCANSO_ENTRE_JORNADAS', 'RODIZIO_DOMINGO_MULHER'/'RODIZIO_DOMINGO_HOMEM' -> 'H3_RODIZIO_DOMINGO' (single case, gender-neutral), 'MAX_JORNADA_DIARIA' -> 'H4_MAX_JORNADA_DIARIA', 'META_SEMANAL' -> 'H10_META_SEMANAL'.

issues_fixed.description: Pin logic in Fase 2 had contradictory double-guard that left a gap for pins with only hora_fim defined.
issues_fixed.fix_applied: Consolidated to single guard: if (pin) continue — any pin (with or without times) prevents folga assignment.

issues_open.description: AP2 (Schedule Instability) uses max-min variation across the entire period, not week-by-week. A colab who works early on week 1 and late on week 2 would be flagged even if each individual week is stable. The RFC spec says 'variacao de hora_inicio > 2h entre dias' which implies within a scheduling window, not globally across the entire month.
issues_open.decision: Accepted as-is for v3.0. Global variation is conservative and produces no false negatives (only false positives on score). Can be refined in a future iteration if score accuracy is a priority.

summary: All issues from QA iteration 1 have been resolved. The critical HIGH bug (tryFixViolation switch with wrong case strings) and the MEDIUM pin logic inconsistency were both fixed. TypeScript check and build pass cleanly with 0 errors after all fixes. The remaining MEDIUM issue (AP2 global vs per-week scope) was accepted as a known limitation for v3.0. Motor v3.1 is approved for production use.
